creator: asmodai@wxs.nl
text:
-On [20000425 23:03], tim@osvif.demon.co.uk (tim@osvif.demon.co.uk) wrote:
>
>My system does not have a CDROM so I am trying to get NFS to another
>machine with a CDROM. The mount always fails with permission denied.
>This never happened in 3.1, 2.2.6, 2.1 and 2.0.

You fail to include the _actual_ error messages.

Also, does the NFS master grant permission to your other machine to let
it even mount the appropriate mountpoint?  [see /etc/exports]

Also, are you trying to mount it as root?

>My system is 32MB RAM, Intel Pentium-MMX 223, Maxtor 84320D4 (4Gb) drive
> and standard floppy disk.
>>How-To-Repeat:
>Just try to install using boot floppies and use NFS.

That works for me.

When submitting problems keep in mind that people are not psychic and
cannot detect what your situation is nor know all circumstances.

--
